title: Traits 2
description: Traits can provide default implementations for methods. Types can override them or use the default.
instructions: Implement the AppendBar trait for Vec<String> with a default implementation.
hints: You need to implement AppendBar for Vec<String>.
The method should push "Bar" onto the vector and return it.
Since self is taken by value, declare it as `mut self` to modify it.
explanation: Traits can be implemented for any type, including standard library types like `Vec<String>`. When a method takes `self` by value, you can add `mut` to make it mutable within the function body: `fn method(mut self) -> Self`. Default implementations in traits let types opt into behavior without writing their own implementation.
